seo.title: LangGraph Guardrails for Education
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangGraph agents in education. Meet FERPA, COPPA requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Education teams using LangGraph build complex AI tutoring agents and adaptive learning workflows that process sensitive student data, making compliance with privacy laws like FERPA and COPPA essential. Cyclic graph executions and state corruption can expose PII or deliver inappropriate content to minors, risking violations and institutional liability. Vex provides runtime guardrails to verify state integrity and content safety in these stateful multi-actor systems.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects student education records from unauthorized disclosure, requiring parental consent for minors under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans graph state for PII leakage during cyclic executions and enforces consent checks before data processing.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates verifiable parental consent for collecting personal information from children under 13.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Intercepts student data flows in parallel graph branches, blocking unverified minor interactions.

complianceRequirements.description: Vary by state but generally require data minimization and audit trails for student interactions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Logs checkpoint states and verifies schema compliance to prevent deserialization errors exposing data.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires educational content and interfaces to be accessible to users with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Validates generated content for WCAG compliance before delivery through graph nodes.

industryRisks.name: Student PII Exposure
industryRisks.description: Cyclic graph loops retain and leak student names, grades, or IDs across sessions.
industryRisks.example: Infinite loop in tutoring graph exposes student transcripts due to unverified state persistence.

industryRisks.name: Inappropriate Content Delivery
industryRisks.description: Parallel branches merge conflicting age-inappropriate or biased responses.
industryRisks.example: Math tutor graph delivers mature examples to elementary students from unfiltered branch.

industryRisks.name: Bypassed Human Review
industryRisks.description: Conditional edges skip human-in-the-loop breakpoints for high-risk interventions.
industryRisks.example: Counseling agent bypasses teacher approval for mental health recommendations.

industryRisks.name: State Schema Drift
industryRisks.description: Checkpoint deserialization fails after curriculum updates, corrupting student progress.
industryRisks.example: New assessment schema breaks legacy student state, losing learning progress data.

implementation.title: Wrap graph.invoke() with Vex Verification
implementation.description: Protect against cyclic execution and PII leakage by verifying inputs/outputs for every invocation.
implementation.code:
import vex
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph

# Sample education graph
graph = StateGraph(StudentState)
# ... add nodes and edges
compiled = graph.compile()

@vex.verify(policies=["ferpa-pii", "coppa-consent", "age-appropriate"])
def safe_invoke(inputs: dict, config: dict):
    result = compiled.invoke(inputs, config)
    # Verify no PII leakage or inappropriate content
    return result

# Usage
response = safe_invoke({"student_id": "123", "query": "explain algebra"}, {"thread_id": "session1"})

implementation.title: Vex Node for State Integrity
implementation.description: Add Vex as a graph node to check state corruption during parallel branch merges.

implementation.title: Human-in-the-Loop with Vex Guardrails
implementation.description: Enforce teacher approval breakpoints that can't be bypassed by conditional edges.

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangGraph in Education: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Maplewood School District deployed LangGraph-powered AI tutors across 5,000 K-12 students to personalize math instruction. The graph featured parallel branches for adaptive difficulty, content generation, and progress tracking, with cyclic re-planning for struggling students. Student data including names, grades, and learning styles flowed through persistent checkpoints across sessions.

Without compliance guardrails, the system hit multiple failure modes: cyclic loops retained PII across student handoffs, parallel branches merged biased content from unfiltered sources, and conditional edges bypassed counselor review for at-risk students flagged by sentiment analysis. During pilot testing, teachers discovered 8th graders receiving college-level examples with mature analogies, violating age-appropriateness rules.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex as both wrapper and graph nodes resolved all issues. Vex blocked 97% of PII leakage attempts during cyclic executions, enforced WCAG accessibility on generated content, and caught biased merge conflicts before student exposure. Teacher approval breakpoints became unbreakable, ensuring human oversight for interventions. The district achieved FERPA compliance certification and expanded deployment district-wide, avoiding $2.4M in potential fines.
